result.summary: networkservices.googleapis.com exposes the Google Cloud Network Services REST API and is also managed via the `gcloud network-services` CLI command group; both use Google Cloud authentication.
result.description: Google Cloud Network Services provides managed networking control-plane resources such as routes, gateways, service bindings, and related traffic-management objects for Google Cloud products. The service endpoint for these APIs is `networkservices.googleapis.com`.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Authenticate with Google Cloud and obtain an access token using the easiest supported path: either run [`gcloud auth login`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) for user credentials or use [Application Default Credentials](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/application-default-credentials). The Network Services API docs state you can use your gcloud CLI credentials or ADC, and method docs require the OAuth scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`.
